title: Noccaea cilicica
intro: Noccaea cilicica, also known as Turkish penny-cress, is a plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and edible leaves. The health benefits of Noccaea cilicica include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include glucosinolates, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anticancer activities, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to its antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Noccaea cilicica is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Noccaea cilicica is classified in the family Brassicaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, Noccaea cilicica is closely related to other plants such as mustard, cabbage, and broccoli. Within the Brassicaceae family, Noccaea cilicica is further classified in the genus Noccaea. The genus Noccaea is a group of plants commonly known as rockcress.
